Objective

Build a supervisor-style remediation system for campaign briefs that can inspect a generated brief, identify blocking validation and quality issues, apply deterministic fixes where possible, perform targeted LLM-driven repairs where necessary, re-run validation, and continue in a controlled loop until the brief is either clean enough for approval or explicitly escalated for manual intervention.

Initial scope

- Prevent "approved" briefs from bypassing unresolved blockers.
- Reuse the existing validation and remediation infrastructure instead of replacing the pipeline wholesale.
- Prioritize one-pass cleanup of the recurring brief failures surfaced in the current campaign set.
- Keep the implementation auditable: each remediation pass should record what failed, what was changed, and why.

Success criteria

- A brief cannot reach approval status while blocking validation issues remain.
- The supervisor can resolve the common structured/content failures without manual prompting.
- Remediation outcomes are visible enough to debug regressions quickly.

Implementation contract

- Input bundle
	- campaign blueprint
	- current aesthetic brief
	- structural validation result
	- existing issue ledger and remediation plan, if present
- Pass order
	- run structural validation
	- convert validation failures into auditable issue-ledger records
	- classify each issue into deterministic fix, llm patch, regenerate, or manual escalation
	- persist a remediation plan that reflects the currently open issues
	- only clear the remediation state when validation returns zero open issues
- Approval rule
	- approval is a terminal supervisor action, not a raw status flip
	- an approved brief must be persisted with an empty open-issue ledger and no active remediation plan
	- any blocker at approval time is a hard error
- Stop conditions
	- stop when validation passes with zero blockers and the brief can move to approval or pending review
	- stop when only manual escalations remain
	- stop when a pass produces no meaningful state change and escalate rather than looping indefinitely

First implementation slice

- Wire the brief engine to persist supervisor-derived issue-ledger and remediation-plan state on create, revise, and approve.
- Use structural validation as the first supervisor signal before layering in red-team or richer repair loops.
- Add regression tests around approval consistency and remediation-plan classification before broader LLM repair work.
